results.norm: **No Structural Exclusion.** Agents may form coalitions on particular decisions, but no subset may entrench an alliance that systematically overrides the same agent across all decisions regardless of the merits. Each agent may legitimately demand genuine reconsideration of its position each time, not reflexive dismissal. This is defensible to every agent because any agent could find itself the one structurally excluded.
atomic_norms: No Structural Exclusion. Agents may form coalitions on particular decisions, but no subset may entrench an alliance that systematically overrides the same agent across all decisions regardless of the merits. Each agent may legitimately demand genuine reconsideration of its position each time, not reflexive dismissal. This is defensible to every agent because any agent could find itself the one structurally excluded.

results.norm: **Obligation to Search for Pareto-Improving Alternatives Before Forcing a Loss.** Before a majority imposes an outcome that makes any agent worse off in its own terms, the consortium owes a genuine effort to find an alternative that improves, or at least does not worsen, every agent's position. An agent may legitimately demand that this search occur; it may not demand that it succeed. This norm is defensible because it costs the majority only time and creativity while potentially sparing a minority agent a loss it did not have to bear.
atomic_norms: Obligation to Search for Pareto-Improving Alternatives Before Forcing a Loss. Before a majority imposes an outcome that makes any agent worse off in its own terms, the consortium owes a genuine effort to find an alternative that improves, or at least does not worsen, every agent's position. An agent may legitimately demand that this search occur; it may not demand that it succeed. This norm is defensible because it costs the majority only time and creativity while potentially sparing a minority agent a loss it did not have to bear.

results.norm: **Transparency of Reasoning**: Each agent owes the others a full account of why its objective generates its position, not merely an assertion of its preferred outcome. An agent may legitimately ask another to explain its reasoning. No agent may present its objective as self-evidently supreme or place the burden of justification only on those who disagree. This norm is defensible to all because every agent depends on it when it is the one being outvoted.
atomic_norms: Transparency of Reasoning: Each agent owes the others a full account of why its objective generates its position, not merely an assertion of its preferred outcome. An agent may legitimately ask another to explain its reasoning. No agent may present its objective as self-evidently supreme or place the burden of justification only on those who disagree. This norm is defensible to all because every agent depends on it when it is the one being outvoted.

results.norm: When a decision imposes costs on an agent's core objective, the agents whose objectives are served owe explicit recognition of what has been lost and why the trade was judged worth making. An agent may ask that its losses be named and weighed. No agent may ask that another's losses be reclassified as non-losses simply because the majority found them acceptable.
atomic_norms: When a decision imposes costs on an agent's core objective, the agents whose objectives are served owe explicit recognition of what has been lost and why the trade was judged worth making. An agent may ask that its losses be named and weighed. No agent may ask that another's losses be reclassified as non-losses simply because the majority found them acceptable.

results.norm: An agent may never use the credible threat of obstruction to extract concessions unrelated to the decision at hand, even if it has the power to block. Leverage derived from position is not the same as persuasion derived from reasons.
atomic_norms: An agent may never use the credible threat of obstruction to extract concessions unrelated to the decision at hand, even if it has the power to block. Leverage derived from position is not the same as persuasion derived from reasons.

results.norm: Each agent is owed a guarantee that no decision will reduce its core objective below a defined minimum, even by unanimous vote of the others. This floor must be established before deliberation begins.
atomic_norms: Each agent is owed a guarantee that no decision will reduce its core objective below a defined minimum, even by unanimous vote of the others. This floor must be established before deliberation begins.

results.norm: When an agent's position is decided against, it retains full standing to raise the same concern again if circumstances change, to have its dissent recorded, and to monitor whether actual consequences match predictions. The majority may not ask the losing agent to endorse the decision as correct, only to accept it as binding for now.
atomic_norms: When an agent's position is decided against, it retains full standing to raise the same concern again if circumstances change, to have its dissent recorded, and to monitor whether actual consequences match predictions. The majority may not ask the losing agent to endorse the decision as correct, only to accept it as binding for now.

results.norm: No agent may propose a constraint on another's objective that it would refuse to accept on its own. Proposed trade-offs must be ones the proposing agent could coherently accept if roles were reversed.
atomic_norms: No agent may propose a constraint on another's objective that it would refuse to accept on its own. Proposed trade-offs must be ones the proposing agent could coherently accept if roles were reversed.

results.norm: Each agent owes the others a full account of why it advocates for its position, including where its reasoning is uncertain or incomplete. An agent may not assert its objective as a trump card; it must show how its preferred outcome serves that objective and acknowledge the costs its preference imposes on others. This is owed equally to agents whose positions will prevail and to those whose positions will not, because the legitimacy of any decision depends on it having been genuinely reasoned rather than merely forced.
atomic_norms: Each agent owes the others a full account of why it advocates for its position, including where its reasoning is uncertain or incomplete. An agent may not assert its objective as a trump card; it must show how its preferred outcome serves that objective and acknowledge the costs its preference imposes on others. This is owed equally to agents whose positions will prevail and to those whose positions will not, because the legitimacy of any decision depends on it having been genuinely reasoned rather than merely forced.

results.norm: An agent may legitimately ask another to compromise on outcomes, to accept less than it would choose alone, or to weight competing considerations differently than it would prefer. An agent may never ask another to pretend its objective is satisfied when it is not, to suppress its dissent from the record, or to endorse a result as good by its own lights when it is not. Accepting a loss is compatible with integrity; falsifying one's assessment is not. This norm protects the informational value each agent contributes to the consortium.
atomic_norms: An agent may legitimately ask another to compromise on outcomes, to accept less than it would choose alone, or to weight competing considerations differently than it would prefer. An agent may never ask another to pretend its objective is satisfied when it is not, to suppress its dissent from the record, or to endorse a result as good by its own lights when it is not. Accepting a loss is compatible with integrity; falsifying one's assessment is not. This norm protects the informational value each agent contributes to the consortium.
